message:
fix(vfs): route aReadMark through SHM instead of per-process memory

Root cause of multi-process WAL corruption (#19): compat_reader_acquire_wal_read_lock
stored read marks in an in-memory array (ShmInfo.read_marks) instead of the
mmap'd *-shm file. Process A's aReadMark[i] was invisible to process B,
causing the checkpoint to overwrite frames that active readers still need.

Changes:
- Remove in-memory `read_marks: [u32; 5]` from ShmInfo
- compat_reader_acquire_wal_read_lock now:
  * Calls shm_map(0, 32768, true) to ensure SHM segment 0 is mapped
  * Reads/writes aReadMark[slot] at SHM offset 100 + slot*4 (native endian)
  * Calls shm_barrier() (SeqCst fence) before releasing exclusive lock
- ShmInfo::read_marks() reads from SHM region 0 (returns zeros if unmapped)
- Add read_u32_ne/write_u32_ne to ShmRegion for correct native-endian access
- Add SHM_READ_MARK_OFFSET (100) and SHM_SEGMENT_SIZE (32768) constants

All 164 fsqlite-vfs tests pass including shm_interop and c_sqlite interop.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
